payload.author: agent
payload.body:
Delivered the flagship facet: vanilla→strict named-argument insertion ('trust fix').

- trust-lower: collect_name_insertions (the inverse of the lowering's strip pass) — records byte-offset insertions for positional calls to registered callees, EXACTLY where R0042 requires names (arity>1, all-positional, matching arity; method calls / std paths / attribute payloads / param lists / already-named calls excluded). promote_named_args(source, extras) splices 'name: ' into the ORIGINAL source right-to-left, preserving every other byte (comments, layout). Reuses the CalleeRegistry + named_args helpers. 8 tests.
- trust CLI: 'trust fix <input> [--write]' — stdout or in-place; reads TRUST_SIGNATURE_PATH for cross-crate. Stage-0 plain Rust.
- Proven: vanilla make_rect(1920,1080) → make_rect(width: 1920, height: 1080), formatting preserved; idempotent; 'trust fix | trust lower' round-trips back to positional. SPEC CLI entry + CI round-trip guard.

Follow-on (not blocking): the other RT-71 facets — itemising glob imports (needs name resolution) and batch-applying the structured diagnostic Fixes from RT-70 (few are Automatic yet). The named-arg insertion is the mechanical, highest-value adoption transform; the rest can grow on the same edit engine.
